entity.name: AI Now Institute
entity.description: NYU-affiliated research institute, ex-Whittaker. Critical-AI / power-concentration framing: emphasizes labor displacement, surveillance, and Big Tech accountability over catastrophic risk. Influences progressive Dem and EU regulatory thinking.

activity.title: Uber For Nursing Part II
activity.raw_text: Three platforms—Clipboard Health, IntelyCare, and ShiftKey—have achieved unicorn status with valuations of $1 billion each. ShiftKey raised more than $300 million from private equity alone, a feat that underscores the anticipated growth of this business model. Our research indicates that this is not a situation in which a few platforms are carving out a space for themselves alongside staffing agencies (and thus emerging as a third player in the field). Instead, gig nursing platforms are in the process of reshaping rules for an entire healthcare industry. Gig nursing platforms now exist in every US state.

_verify_phrases: gig nursing platforms are in the process of reshaping rules

enrichment.summary: AI Now Institute published a policy paper examining the rise of gig nursing platforms—Clipboard Health, IntelyCare, and ShiftKey—which have each achieved unicorn valuations of $1 billion and now operate in every US state. The report argues these platforms are not merely a niche supplement to traditional staffing agencies but are actively reshaping labor rules across the entire healthcare industry. The paper appears to be a follow-up to an earlier report on the same subject, extending analysis of the platformization of nursing work.
enrichment.stance_quote: gig nursing platforms are in the process of reshaping rules for an entire healthcare industry

activity.title: Reframing Impact: AI Summit 2026 (series)
activity.raw_text: The 2026 AI Impact Summit in India is the latest iteration of an event that has become a bellwether for global discourse around the AI industry, especially the question of whether, and how, it can be governed. In the aftermath of the "AI boom," this report examines how the push to integrate AI products everywhere grants AI companies - and the tech oligarchs that run them - power that goes far beyond their deep pockets.

_verify_phrases: grants AI companies - and the tech oligarchs that run them - power

enrichment.summary: AI Now Institute published a policy paper examining the 2026 AI Impact Summit in India as a lens for analyzing global AI governance discourse. The report critiques the integration of AI products across sectors as a mechanism by which AI companies and their leadership accumulate disproportionate societal power. It frames the summit as a bellwether for debates over whether and how the AI industry can be governed.
enrichment.stance_quote: the push to integrate AI products everywhere grants AI companies - and the tech oligarchs that run them - power that goes far beyond their deep pockets.

activity.title: Reframing Impact: Open Source (with Meredith Whittaker)
activity.raw_text: Meredith Whittaker is the President of Signal. Her research and advocacy focus on the social implications of artificial intelligence and the tech industry responsible for it, with a particular emphasis on power and the political economy driving the commercialization of computational technology. In this conversation, Whittaker unpacks "open-source AI." In the context of software, "open source" referred to a set of precise technical protocols and processes that were arguably decentralizing. With AI, Whittaker says, this is not the case. Open-source AI, she argues, is not technical so much as vibes-based.

_verify_phrases: Open-source AI, she argues, is not technical so much as vibes-based

enrichment.summary: The AI Now Institute published a policy paper featuring a conversation with Meredith Whittaker, President of Signal, on the concept of "open-source AI." Whittaker argues that unlike traditional open-source software, which involved precise technical protocols with arguably decentralizing effects, "open-source AI" lacks comparable technical rigor and is instead "vibes-based." The piece situates this critique within a broader analysis of power and the political economy driving AI commercialization.
enrichment.stance_quote: Open-source AI, she argues, is not technical so much as vibes-based.

activity.title: Reframing Impact: Multilateralism (with Chinasa T. Okolo)
activity.raw_text: Chinasa T. Okolo is the founder of Technecultura and a policy specialist at the United Nations Office for Digital and Emerging Technologies (ODET). Her research examines how African governments can effectuate robust AI and data governance, investigates the geopolitical impacts of AI, and analyzes datafication and algorithmic marginalization in Africa. In this conversation, Okolo unpacks the current state of AI global governance, and Big Tech's dominance, from a Majority World perspective.

_verify_phrases: Okolo unpacks the current state of AI global governance

enrichment.summary: AI Now Institute published a policy paper featuring a conversation with Chinasa T. Okolo, founder of Technecultura and a UN policy specialist, examining AI global governance from a Majority World perspective. The piece focuses on African AI and data governance, the geopolitical impacts of AI, and Big Tech's dominance in international AI governance structures. Okolo discusses datafication and algorithmic marginalization in Africa and the current state of multilateral AI governance.
enrichment.stance_quote: Okolo unpacks the current state of AI global governance, and Big Tech's dominance, from a Majority World perspective.

activity.title: Reframing Impact: Sovereignty (with Rafael Grohmann)
activity.raw_text: In this conversation, Grohmann unpacks digital sovereignty. He historicizes the concept, tracing it to 1970s anti-imperialist political economy, which examined how global power and economic dependence shaped national development. In this series—produced by AI Now Institute, Aapti Institute, and The Maybe—we bring together leading advocates, builders, and thinkers from around the world who live and breathe substance, analysis, and meaningful action into these ideas.

enrichment.summary: AI Now Institute published a policy paper conversation with Rafael Grohmann examining the concept of digital sovereignty. The piece traces the concept's origins to 1970s anti-imperialist political economy and its focus on how global power and economic dependence shaped national development. It is part of a series co-produced by AI Now Institute, Aapti Institute, and The Maybe, featuring advocates and thinkers on these themes.
